metadata.source_folder: Builder Staples (curated for the 90-day muscle program)
metadata.notes: Grazer-friendly, calorie-dense staples with verified macros. These make the Weekly Builder's snack/shake slots and protein floor solvable. Macros are per serving.

recipes.name: The Builder Shake
recipes.nutrition_full: Per shake: 660 kcal, 61 g protein, 71 g carbohydrate, 18 g fat. Verified from standard ingredient data.
recipes.description: The mid-morning anchor: 660 drinkable calories in ninety seconds. Add 5 g creatine monohydrate daily - it is flavorless here.
instructions: Add everything to a blender, liquids first.
Optional daily add-in: 5 g creatine monohydrate.
tips: Swap water for whole milk to add roughly 150 kcal on hard training days.

recipes.name: Quick Protein Shake
recipes.nutrition_full: Per shake: 290 kcal, 44 g protein, 16 g carbohydrate, 7 g fat.
recipes.description: The protein-gap closer - light on calories, heavy on protein. The planner reaches for this when a day runs protein-short.

recipes.name: Greek Yogurt Power Bowl
recipes.nutrition_full: Per bowl: 510 kcal, 26 g protein, 69 g carbohydrate, 15 g fat.
recipes.description: Fast breakfast or afternoon fuel - no cooking, one bowl.
instructions: Layer yogurt, granola, and berries in a bowl.
tips: Stir in half a scoop of whey to lift protein to ~38 g.

recipes.name: Cottage Cheese and Pineapple Cup
recipes.nutrition_full: Per cup: 260 kcal, 25 g protein, 19 g carbohydrate, 10 g fat.
recipes.description: Slow-digesting casein protein in snack form - ideal afternoon or evening.

recipes.name: Overnight Chia Protein Oats
recipes.nutrition_full: Per jar: 380 kcal, 33 g protein, 40 g carbohydrate, 10 g fat.
recipes.description: Assembled the night before - breakfast is waiting when you wake up.
ingredients: 1 scoop whey or casein protein powder
instructions: Stir everything together in a jar.
Refrigerate overnight; eat cold or warmed.
tips: Top with berries or half a sliced banana for ~50 extra kcal.

recipes.name: Chicken, Rice and Broccoli Plate
recipes.nutrition_full: Per plate: 560 kcal, 60 g protein, 51 g carbohydrate, 11 g fat.
recipes.description: The classic for a reason. Batch-cook five portions on Sunday and lunch is solved through Friday.
ingredients: 1 cup cooked white or brown rice
Salt, pepper, and any seasoning you like
instructions: Season chicken and grill or pan-cook until 165F inside.
Serve over rice with steamed broccoli; finish with olive oil and seasoning.
tips: Batch-cook: 2 lb chicken + 3 cups dry rice makes five plates.

recipes.name: Tuna and Avocado Power Toast
recipes.nutrition_full: Per serving: 490 kcal, 35 g protein, 48 g carbohydrate, 19 g fat.
recipes.description: Five-minute desk lunch with real protein and healthy fats.
instructions: Mash avocado with lemon, salt, and pepper.
Spread on toast, pile tuna on top.

recipes.name: Peanut Butter Banana Toast
recipes.nutrition_full: Per serving: 505 kcal, 16 g protein, 72 g carbohydrate, 19 g fat.
recipes.description: Calorie density on demand - the easiest 500 calories in the kitchen, best before or after training.
tips: Drizzle of honey adds ~60 kcal when the ramp steps up.

recipes.name: Trail Mix Scoop
recipes.nutrition_full: Per scoop: 380 kcal, 9 g protein, 26 g carbohydrate, 29 g fat.
recipes.description: Pocket calories for the grazer - no prep, no refrigeration, lives in the desk drawer.
instructions: Pre-portion into small bags or containers - eyeballing trail mix is how surpluses get away from you.

recipes.name: Casein Night Pudding
recipes.nutrition_full: Per serving: 320 kcal, 44 g protein, 18 g carbohydrate, 9 g fat.
recipes.description: Slow-release protein before bed - feeds overnight muscle repair, which is where the 80/20 book says growth actually happens.
instructions: Stir milk into powder gradually until pudding-thick.
Stir in chia; rest 5 minutes (or refrigerate) and eat.
